---
title: "Framed Listening: NLP Model Comparison — Top & Bottom Semantic Similarity Pairs"
author: "Hazel A. van der Walle (PhD student, Music, Durham University)"
date: "23 April 2026"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    theme: cosmo
    self-contained: true
---

All datasets generated and used for this study are openly available on GitHub:
<https://github.com/HazelvdW/context-framed-listening>.

This document runs all six NLP models on **both** data levels — aggregated combMIMC
documents (Group A) and individual MIMC responses (Group B) — to provide a fully
comparable cross-model, cross-level view for model selection decisions.

**Why both levels for all models?**

Running every model on both levels reveals two things that a partial comparison
cannot: (1) whether a model's behaviour changes substantially with input length
and aggregation, and (2) whether the performance advantages of sentence-embedding
models over TF-IDF/Word2Vec are consistent across scales. TF-IDF and Word2Vec are
expected to perform poorly at the individual level due to sparse lexical overlap. 
Seeing that failure empirically, alongside the scores each model assigns to the
same conceptually similar pairs, is the evidence needed to justify model choices.

> **Cross-level similarity scores are not directly comparable** — the units differ
> (64 documents vs many individual responses). Cross-model comparisons are run
> within each group separately. The distributions section gives the best high-level
> view of how each model behaves across both levels.

**All models × both levels:**

| | TF-IDF | W2V `lvl2a` | W2V `lvl1a` | BERT | MiniLM | mpnet | para-mpnet |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| **A — combMIMC** | A1 | A2 | A3 | A4 | A5 | A6 | A7 |
| **B — individual** | B1 | B2 | B3 | B4 | B5 | B6 | B7 |

---

## Setup

```{r setup-r, echo=FALSE}
library(reticulate)

# Uncomment to install on first run:
 reticulate::py_install(c(
   "pandas", "numpy", "scikit-learn", "scipy",
   "matplotlib", "seaborn", "sentence-transformers",
   "torch", "gensim"
 ), pip = TRUE)
```

```{python setup, echo=FALSE}
import os
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer
from sklearn.metrics.pairwise         import cosine_similarity
from sentence_transformers            import SentenceTransformer, models as st_models
import gensim.downloader as api
from gensim.models                    import KeyedVectors

base_path  = (
    "/Users/hazelaileenvanderwalle/Library/CloudStorage/"
    "OneDrive-Personal/work/study/PhD/_PROJECT/"
    "_framedListening_NLP/context-framed-listening"
)
output_dir = f"{base_path}/NLP_outputs/model_comparison"
os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

TOP_N     = 10
MAX_CHARS = 300
MIN_WORDS = 5     # pairs where either original text has fewer words than this are excluded

print(f"Output directory: {output_dir}")
print(f"Showing top/bottom {TOP_N} pairs per model")
```

```{python version-check, echo=FALSE}
# ── Environment version check ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
import importlib, sys
from packaging.version import Version

REQUIREMENTS = {
    "numpy":                "1.26",
    "pandas":               "2.1",
    "sklearn":              "1.3",    # scikit-learn
    "matplotlib":           "3.8",
    "seaborn":              "0.13",
    "sentence_transformers": "2.7",   # sentence-transformers
    "torch":                "2.1",
    "gensim":               "4.3",
}

print(f"Python {sys.version}")
print("-" * 52)
all_ok = True
for pkg, min_ver in REQUIREMENTS.items():
    try:
        mod = importlib.import_module(pkg)
        ver = getattr(mod, "__version__", "unknown")
        ok  = Version(ver) >= Version(min_ver) if ver != "unknown" else True
        status = "OK" if ok else f"WARNING — expected >={min_ver}"
        if not ok:
            all_ok = False
        print(f"  {pkg:<26} {ver:<12} {status}")
    except ImportError:
        print(f"  {pkg:<26} NOT INSTALLED  ERROR")
        all_ok = False
if all_ok:
    print("\nAll package versions OK.")
else:
    print("\nOne or more issues — run: pip install -r requirements.txt")
```

---

## Load Data

```{python load-data, echo=FALSE}
# ── Group A: aggregated combMIMC documents (N = 64) ───────────────────────────
df_comblvl2a = pd.read_csv(f"{base_path}/NLP_outputs/combMIMC_lvl2a.csv")
df_comblvl1a = pd.read_csv(f"{base_path}/NLP_outputs/combMIMC_lvl1a.csv")

if "text_original" not in df_comblvl2a.columns or "text_original" not in df_comblvl1a.columns:
    raise ValueError(
        "combMIMC CSV files are missing 'text_original'.\n"
        "Re-run NLP-text-prep.qmd to regenerate them."
    )
orig_col_comb = "text_original"

print(f"combMIMC_lvl2a: {len(df_comblvl2a)} documents  |  columns: {df_comblvl2a.columns.tolist()}")
print(f"combMIMC_lvl1a: {len(df_comblvl1a)} documents  |  columns: {df_comblvl1a.columns.tolist()}")

# ── Group B: individual MIMC responses ────────────────────────────────────────
df_indiv = pd.read_csv(f"{base_path}/NLP_outputs/dataMIMC_preprocessed.csv")

orig_col_indiv = next(
    (c for c in ["descr_THOUGHT.text_spellchecked", "descr_THOUGHT.text"]
     if c in df_indiv.columns), None)
if orig_col_indiv is None:
    raise ValueError("Cannot find original text column in dataMIMC_preprocessed.csv.")

df_indiv = df_indiv.dropna(
    subset=["textMIMC_lvl1a", "textMIMC_lvl2a", orig_col_indiv]
).reset_index(drop=True)

print(f"\ndataMIMC_preprocessed: {len(df_indiv)} individual responses")
print(f"  Original text column: '{orig_col_indiv}'")
```

---

## Helper Functions

```{python helpers, echo=FALSE}
def truncate(text, max_chars=MAX_CHARS):
    text = str(text)
    return text if len(text) <= max_chars else text[:max_chars] + "…"


def build_pairs_df(sim_matrix, source_df, model_col, original_col):
    """Build a long-format pairs DataFrame, excluding:
      - pairs where both original texts are identical
      - pairs where either original text is shorter than MIN_WORDS words
      - same-participant same-cell pairs (if PROLIFIC_PID is present)
    """
    genre_col_local = next(
        (c for c in ["genre_code", "clip_genre"] if c in source_df.columns), None)
    pid_col   = "PROLIFIC_PID" if "PROLIFIC_PID" in source_df.columns else None
    orig_vals = source_df[original_col].astype(str).str.strip().values
    word_counts = np.array([len(v.split()) for v in orig_vals])
    n = sim_matrix.shape[0]
    n_skip_id, n_skip_pid, n_skip_short = 0, 0, 0
    rows = []
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(i + 1, n):
            if orig_vals[i] == orig_vals[j]:
                n_skip_id += 1
                continue
            if word_counts[i] < MIN_WORDS or word_counts[j] < MIN_WORDS:
                n_skip_short += 1
                continue
            if pid_col and (
                source_df[pid_col].iloc[i] == source_df[pid_col].iloc[j] and
                source_df["clip_name"].iloc[i]    == source_df["clip_name"].iloc[j] and
                source_df["context_word"].iloc[i] == source_df["context_word"].iloc[j]
            ):
                n_skip_pid += 1
                continue
            row = dict(
                idx_a=i, idx_b=j,
                similarity=float(sim_matrix[i, j]),
                text_a_model=truncate(source_df[model_col].iloc[i]),
                text_b_model=truncate(source_df[model_col].iloc[j]),
                text_a_orig=truncate(source_df[original_col].iloc[i]),
                text_b_orig=truncate(source_df[original_col].iloc[j]),
                clip_a=source_df["clip_name"].iloc[i],
                clip_b=source_df["clip_name"].iloc[j],
                context_a=source_df["context_word"].iloc[i],
                context_b=source_df["context_word"].iloc[j],
                same_clip=(source_df["clip_name"].iloc[i] == source_df["clip_name"].iloc[j]),
                same_context=(source_df["context_word"].iloc[i] == source_df["context_word"].iloc[j]),
            )
            if genre_col_local:
                row["genre_a"] = source_df[genre_col_local].iloc[i]
                row["genre_b"] = source_df[genre_col_local].iloc[j]
            rows.append(row)
    pairs = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values("similarity", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
    print(f"  Pairs: {len(pairs):,}  |  skipped — identical: {n_skip_id:,}  |  "
          f"short (<{MIN_WORDS} words): {n_skip_short:,}  |  "
          f"same person/cell: {n_skip_pid:,}")
    return pairs


def top_bottom(pairs_df, n=TOP_N):
    return pairs_df.head(n), pairs_df.tail(n).sort_values("similarity")


def print_pairs(pairs_df):
    for rank, (_, row) in enumerate(pairs_df.iterrows(), start=1):
        print(f"\n  [{rank}]  Sim={row['similarity']:.4f}  |  "
              f"{'✓ same clip' if row['same_clip'] else '✗ diff clip'}  "
              f"{'✓ same context' if row['same_context'] else '✗ diff context'}")
        print(f"       {row['clip_a']} / {row['context_a']}  vs  "
              f"{row['clip_b']} / {row['context_b']}")
        print(f"  ── Original A: {row['text_a_orig']}")
        print(f"  ── Original B: {row['text_b_orig']}")
        if row["text_a_model"] != row["text_a_orig"]:
            print(f"     Model A:    {row['text_a_model']}")
            print(f"     Model B:    {row['text_b_model']}")


def plot_pairs(top_df, bot_df, model_name, slug):
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(20, max(6, TOP_N * 0.9)))
    for ax, data, title, color in [
        (axes[0], top_df, f"Top {TOP_N} Most Similar",    "#27ae60"),
        (axes[1], bot_df, f"Bottom {TOP_N} Least Similar", "#e74c3c"),
    ]:
        labels = [f"A: {r['text_a_orig'][:60]}…\nB: {r['text_b_orig'][:60]}…"
                  for _, r in data.iterrows()]
        sims = data["similarity"].values
        ax.barh(range(len(sims)), sims, color=color, alpha=0.8,
                edgecolor="black", linewidth=0.6)
        ax.set_yticks(range(len(sims)))
        ax.set_yticklabels(labels, fontsize=7.5)
        ax.set_xlabel("Cosine Similarity", fontsize=10)
        ax.set_title(title, fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
        ax.set_xlim([0, 1])
        ax.grid(axis="x", alpha=0.3)
        for i, sim in enumerate(sims):
            ax.text(sim + 0.01, i, f"{sim:.3f}", va="center", fontsize=8)
    plt.suptitle(model_name, fontsize=13, fontweight="bold")
    plt.tight_layout()
    path = f"{output_dir}/pairs_{slug}.png"
    plt.savefig(path, dpi=200, bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.show()
    print(f"Saved: {path}")


def save_pairs(top_df, bot_df, slug):
    top_df.assign(rank_type="top").to_csv(
        f"{output_dir}/pairs_top_{slug}.csv", index=False)
    bot_df.assign(rank_type="bottom").to_csv(
        f"{output_dir}/pairs_bottom_{slug}.csv", index=False)


def pair_set(df_p):
    return set(zip(df_p["idx_a"].astype(int), df_p["idx_b"].astype(int)))


def jaccard(a, b):
    return len(a & b) / len(a | b) if (a or b) else 1.0


def run_cross_model(model_results, sim_lookup, group_label, ref_key,
                    dist_title, n_cols=4):
    """Run all three cross-model visualisations for one group."""
    names = list(model_results.keys())

    # 1. Jaccard overlap heatmaps (top and bottom)
    for which, idx in [("Top", 0), ("Bottom", 1)]:
        mat = np.zeros((len(names), len(names)))
        for i, m1 in enumerate(names):
            for j, m2 in enumerate(names):
                mat[i, j] = jaccard(pair_set(model_results[m1][idx]),
                                     pair_set(model_results[m2][idx]))
        n = len(names)
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(max(7, n * 1.1), max(6, n)))
        sns.heatmap(mat, annot=True, fmt=".2f", cmap="YlGn",
                    xticklabels=names, yticklabels=names,
                    vmin=0, vmax=1, linewidths=0.5, linecolor="gray", ax=ax)
        ax.set_title(f"Group {group_label} — Jaccard Overlap ({which} {TOP_N} pairs)\n"
                     "1.0 = identical pair sets, 0.0 = no overlap",
                     fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
        plt.xticks(rotation=30, ha="right", fontsize=9)
        plt.tight_layout()
        path = f"{output_dir}/group{group_label}_jaccard_{which.lower()}.png"
        plt.savefig(path, dpi=200, bbox_inches="tight")
        plt.show()
        print(f"Saved: {path}")

    # 2. Score distributions
    all_sims = {k: sm[np.triu_indices_from(sm, k=1)] for k, sm in sim_lookup.items()}
    n_rows   = (len(names) + n_cols - 1) // n_cols
    colors   = plt.cm.tab10(np.linspace(0, 0.9, len(names)))
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(n_rows, n_cols,
                              figsize=(n_cols * 5.5, n_rows * 4.5),
                              squeeze=False)
    axes_flat = axes.flatten()
    for ax, (name, sims), color in zip(axes_flat, all_sims.items(), colors):
        ax.hist(sims, bins=40, color=color, alpha=0.8,
                edgecolor="black", linewidth=0.4)
        ax.axvline(sims.mean(), color="black", linestyle="--", linewidth=1.5,
                   label=f"Mean: {sims.mean():.3f}")
        ax.axvline(np.median(sims), color="white", linestyle=":", linewidth=1.5,
                   label=f"Median: {np.median(sims):.3f}")
        ax.set_title(name, fontsize=10, fontweight="bold")
        ax.set_xlabel("Cosine Similarity", fontsize=9)
        ax.set_ylabel("Count", fontsize=9)
        ax.legend(fontsize=8)
        ax.text(0.97, 0.92, f"SD={sims.std():.3f}\n"
                f"[{sims.min():.2f}, {sims.max():.2f}]",
                ha="right", va="top", transform=ax.transAxes, fontsize=8,
                bbox=dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.3", facecolor="white", alpha=0.8))
        ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.3)
    for ax in axes_flat[len(names):]:
        ax.axis("off")
    plt.suptitle(dist_title, fontsize=13, fontweight="bold")
    plt.tight_layout()
    path = f"{output_dir}/group{group_label}_distributions.png"
    plt.savefig(path, dpi=200, bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.show()
    print(f"Saved: {path}")

    # Printed summary stats table
    print(f"\nSUMMARY STATISTICS — Group {group_label}")
    print(f"{'Model':<22} {'Mean':>8} {'Median':>8} {'SD':>8} {'Min':>8} {'Max':>8}")
    print("-" * 66)
    for name, sims in all_sims.items():
        print(f"  {name:<20} {sims.mean():>8.4f} {np.median(sims):>8.4f} "
              f"{sims.std():>8.4f} {sims.min():>8.4f} {sims.max():>8.4f}")

    # 3. Side-by-side score heatmap using ref_key's top/bottom as reference
    ref_pairs = model_results[ref_key]
    ref_top   = ref_pairs[0].head(TOP_N).copy()
    ref_bot   = ref_pairs[1].tail(TOP_N).copy()
    ref_both  = pd.concat([ref_top, ref_bot]).reset_index(drop=True)
    ref_both["rank_type"] = (["top"] * TOP_N) + (["bottom"] * TOP_N)

    score_cols = list(sim_lookup.keys())
    rows = []
    for _, row in ref_both.iterrows():
        ia, ib = int(row["idx_a"]), int(row["idx_b"])
        entry  = dict(rank_type=row["rank_type"],
                      text_a_orig=row["text_a_orig"],
                      text_b_orig=row["text_b_orig"])
        for mname, mat in sim_lookup.items():
            entry[mname] = round(float(mat[ia, ib]), 4)
        rows.append(entry)

    comp_df = pd.DataFrame(rows)
    comp_df.to_csv(f"{output_dir}/group{group_label}_cross_model_scores.csv", index=False)

    score_mat   = comp_df[score_cols].values
    pair_labels = [
        f"[{r['rank_type'].upper()}] "
        f"A: {r['text_a_orig'][:40]}… | B: {r['text_b_orig'][:40]}…"
        for _, r in comp_df.iterrows()
    ]
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(max(12, len(score_cols) * 1.8),
                                    max(8, len(pair_labels) * 0.5)))
    sns.heatmap(score_mat, annot=True, fmt=".3f", cmap="RdYlGn",
                xticklabels=score_cols, yticklabels=pair_labels,
                vmin=0, vmax=1, linewidths=0.3, linecolor="gray",
                annot_kws={"size": 8}, ax=ax)
    ax.set_title(
        f"Group {group_label} — Cross-Model Similarity Scores\n"
        f"(Reference pairs = {ref_key}'s top/bottom {TOP_N})",
        fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
    ax.set_xticklabels(ax.get_xticklabels(), rotation=30, ha="right", fontsize=9)
    ax.set_yticklabels(ax.get_yticklabels(), rotation=0, fontsize=7)
    plt.tight_layout()
    path = f"{output_dir}/group{group_label}_cross_model_heatmap.png"
    plt.savefig(path, dpi=200, bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.show()
    print(f"Saved: {path}")

    # Readable print
    print(f"\nCROSS-MODEL SCORES — Group {group_label} "
          f"(reference: {ref_key} top/bottom {TOP_N} pairs)")
    print("=" * 80)
    for _, row in comp_df.iterrows():
        print(f"\n  [{row['rank_type'].upper()}]")
        print(f"  A: {row['text_a_orig']}")
        print(f"  B: {row['text_b_orig']}")
        for m in score_cols:
            print(f"    {m:<30} {row[m]:.4f}")

    return all_sims


print("Helper functions defined.")
```

---

# Group A — Aggregated combMIMC (N = 64)

---

## A1 — TF-IDF

**Data**: `combMIMC_lvl2a.csv`  |  **Preprocessing**: `lvl2a` (aggressive)

Bag-of-words similarity: texts are similar only if they share rare words. No
understanding of meaning — "furious" and "angry" score zero similarity. Expected
to be most meaningful at the aggregated level where documents are long enough for
word overlap to be informative.

```{python a1-tfidf, echo=FALSE}
print("=" * 65)
print("A1 — TF-IDF  (combMIMC_lvl2a)")
print("=" * 65)

vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(min_df=1, max_df=1.0, sublinear_tf=True)
tfidf_mat  = vectorizer.fit_transform(df_comblvl2a["textMIMC_lvl2a"])
sm_A_tfidf = cosine_similarity(tfidf_mat)

pairs_A_tfidf          = build_pairs_df(sm_A_tfidf, df_comblvl2a,
                                         "textMIMC_lvl2a", orig_col_comb)
top_A_tfidf, bot_A_tfidf = top_bottom(pairs_A_tfidf)

print(f"\nTOP {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(top_A_tfidf)
print(f"\nBOTTOM {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(bot_A_tfidf)
plot_pairs(top_A_tfidf, bot_A_tfidf, "A1 — TF-IDF (combMIMC_lvl2a)", "A1_tfidf")
save_pairs(top_A_tfidf, bot_A_tfidf, "A1_tfidf")
```

---

## A2 — Word2Vec (`lvl2a`)

**Data**: `combMIMC_lvl2a.csv`  |  **Preprocessing**: `lvl2a` (aggressive)

Static word embeddings averaged per document. Captures semantic relatedness
("furious" ≈ "angry") unlike TF-IDF, but loses word order and sentence structure.

> Pre-trained `word2vec-google-news-300` vectors (~1.6 GB). Downloads once and caches.

```{python a2-w2v-lvl2a, echo=FALSE}
print("=" * 65)
print("A2 — Word2Vec lvl2a  (combMIMC_lvl2a)")
print("=" * 65)

w2v_cache = f"{output_dir}/word2vec_google_news_300.bin"
if os.path.exists(w2v_cache):
    w2v_model = KeyedVectors.load(w2v_cache)
    print("Loaded Word2Vec from cache.")
else:
    print("Downloading word2vec-google-news-300 (~1.6 GB) ...")
    w2v_model = api.load("word2vec-google-news-300")
    w2v_model.save(w2v_cache)
    print(f"Saved: {w2v_cache}")

def text_to_w2v(text, model, dim=300):
    tokens = str(text).lower().split()
    vecs   = [model[w] for w in tokens if w in model]
    return np.mean(vecs, axis=0) if vecs else np.zeros(dim)

def embed_w2v(texts, model):
    embs  = np.vstack([text_to_w2v(t, model) for t in texts])
    norms = np.linalg.norm(embs, axis=1, keepdims=True)
    norms[norms == 0] = 1
    n_zero = int((np.linalg.norm(embs, axis=1) == 0).sum())
    return embs / norms, n_zero

emb, nz = embed_w2v(df_comblvl2a["textMIMC_lvl2a"], w2v_model)
if nz: print(f"Warning: {nz} zero vectors.")
sm_A_w2v_lvl2a = cosine_similarity(emb)

pairs_A_w2v_lvl2a          = build_pairs_df(sm_A_w2v_lvl2a, df_comblvl2a,
                                              "textMIMC_lvl2a", orig_col_comb)
top_A_w2v_lvl2a, bot_A_w2v_lvl2a = top_bottom(pairs_A_w2v_lvl2a)

print(f"\nTOP {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(top_A_w2v_lvl2a)
print(f"\nBOTTOM {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(bot_A_w2v_lvl2a)
plot_pairs(top_A_w2v_lvl2a, bot_A_w2v_lvl2a,
           "A2 — Word2Vec lvl2a (combMIMC_lvl2a)", "A2_w2v_lvl2a")
save_pairs(top_A_w2v_lvl2a, bot_A_w2v_lvl2a, "A2_w2v_lvl2a")
```

---

## A3 — Word2Vec (`lvl1a`)

**Data**: `combMIMC_lvl1a.csv`  |  **Preprocessing**: `lvl1a` (light)

Same model as A2 on lighter-preprocessed text. Comparing A2 vs A3 isolates the
preprocessing effect on Word2Vec. Comparing A3 vs A4 (BERT) isolates contextualisation,
since both receive identical `lvl1a` input.

```{python a3-w2v-lvl1a, echo=FALSE}
print("=" * 65)
print("A3 — Word2Vec lvl1a  (combMIMC_lvl1a)")
print("=" * 65)

emb, nz = embed_w2v(df_comblvl1a["textMIMC_lvl1a"], w2v_model)
if nz: print(f"Warning: {nz} zero vectors.")
sm_A_w2v_lvl1a = cosine_similarity(emb)

pairs_A_w2v_lvl1a          = build_pairs_df(sm_A_w2v_lvl1a, df_comblvl1a,
                                              "textMIMC_lvl1a", orig_col_comb)
top_A_w2v_lvl1a, bot_A_w2v_lvl1a = top_bottom(pairs_A_w2v_lvl1a)

print(f"\nTOP {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(top_A_w2v_lvl1a)
print(f"\nBOTTOM {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(bot_A_w2v_lvl1a)
plot_pairs(top_A_w2v_lvl1a, bot_A_w2v_lvl1a,
           "A3 — Word2Vec lvl1a (combMIMC_lvl1a)", "A3_w2v_lvl1a")
save_pairs(top_A_w2v_lvl1a, bot_A_w2v_lvl1a, "A3_w2v_lvl1a")
```

---

## A4 — BERT

**Data**: `combMIMC_lvl1a.csv`  |  **Preprocessing**: `lvl1a` (light)

Contextualised embeddings with mean-pooling. Not fine-tuned for similarity, so
the embedding space may not be optimally structured for cosine comparison — but
it captures word meaning in context, unlike A3 (Word2Vec).

```{python a4-bert, echo=FALSE}
print("=" * 65)
print("A4 — BERT  (combMIMC_lvl1a)")
print("=" * 65)

cache = f"{output_dir}/embeddings_A_bert.npy"
if os.path.exists(cache):
    emb = np.load(cache); print(f"Loaded from cache: {emb.shape}")
else:
    we = st_models.Transformer("bert-base-uncased", max_seq_length=512)
    pl = st_models.Pooling(we.get_word_embedding_dimension(),
                            pooling_mode_mean_tokens=True)
    m  = SentenceTransformer(modules=[we, pl])
    emb = m.encode(df_comblvl1a["textMIMC_lvl1a"].tolist(), batch_size=16,
                   show_progress_bar=True, convert_to_numpy=True,
                   normalize_embeddings=True)
    np.save(cache, emb); print(f"Saved: {cache}")

sm_A_bert = cosine_similarity(emb)
pairs_A_bert          = build_pairs_df(sm_A_bert, df_comblvl1a,
                                        "textMIMC_lvl1a", orig_col_comb)
top_A_bert, bot_A_bert = top_bottom(pairs_A_bert)

print(f"\nTOP {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(top_A_bert)
print(f"\nBOTTOM {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(bot_A_bert)
plot_pairs(top_A_bert, bot_A_bert, "A4 — BERT (combMIMC_lvl1a)", "A4_bert")
save_pairs(top_A_bert, bot_A_bert, "A4_bert")
```

---

## A5 — all-MiniLM-L6-v2

**Data**: `combMIMC_lvl1a.csv`  |  **Preprocessing**: `lvl1a` (light)

Fine-tuned for semantic similarity on 1B+ sentence pairs. The embedding space is
trained so cosine similarity directly reflects semantic closeness. Running on
combMIMC shows whether similarity fine-tuning changes behaviour at document scale
vs individual scale (compare A5 vs B5).

```{python a5-minilm, echo=FALSE}
print("=" * 65)
print("A5 — all-MiniLM-L6-v2  (combMIMC_lvl1a)")
print("=" * 65)

cache = f"{output_dir}/embeddings_A_minilm.npy"
if os.path.exists(cache):
    emb = np.load(cache); print(f"Loaded from cache: {emb.shape}")
else:
    m = SentenceTransformer("sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2")
    m.max_seq_length = 128
    emb = m.encode(df_comblvl1a["textMIMC_lvl1a"].tolist(), batch_size=64,
                   show_progress_bar=True, convert_to_numpy=True,
                   normalize_embeddings=True)
    np.save(cache, emb); print(f"Saved: {cache}")

sm_A_minilm = cosine_similarity(emb)
pairs_A_minilm          = build_pairs_df(sm_A_minilm, df_comblvl1a,
                                          "textMIMC_lvl1a", orig_col_comb)
top_A_minilm, bot_A_minilm = top_bottom(pairs_A_minilm)

print(f"\nTOP {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(top_A_minilm)
print(f"\nBOTTOM {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(bot_A_minilm)
plot_pairs(top_A_minilm, bot_A_minilm,
           "A5 — all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (combMIMC_lvl1a)", "A5_minilm")
save_pairs(top_A_minilm, bot_A_minilm, "A5_minilm")
```

---

## A6 — all-mpnet-base-v2

**Data**: `combMIMC_lvl1a.csv`  |  **Preprocessing**: `lvl1a` (light)

Highest-quality general-purpose sentence-embedding model. 768-dimensional
embeddings, max 512 tokens. Quality ceiling for the sentence-transformer family.

```{python a6-mpnet, echo=FALSE}
print("=" * 65)
print("A6 — all-mpnet-base-v2  (combMIMC_lvl1a)")
print("=" * 65)

cache = f"{output_dir}/embeddings_A_mpnet.npy"
if os.path.exists(cache):
    emb = np.load(cache); print(f"Loaded from cache: {emb.shape}")
else:
    m = SentenceTransformer("sentence-transformers/all-mpnet-base-v2")
    m.max_seq_length = 512
    emb = m.encode(df_comblvl1a["textMIMC_lvl1a"].tolist(), batch_size=32,
                   show_progress_bar=True, convert_to_numpy=True,
                   normalize_embeddings=True)
    np.save(cache, emb); print(f"Saved: {cache}")

sm_A_mpnet = cosine_similarity(emb)
pairs_A_mpnet          = build_pairs_df(sm_A_mpnet, df_comblvl1a,
                                         "textMIMC_lvl1a", orig_col_comb)
top_A_mpnet, bot_A_mpnet = top_bottom(pairs_A_mpnet)

print(f"\nTOP {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(top_A_mpnet)
print(f"\nBOTTOM {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(bot_A_mpnet)
plot_pairs(top_A_mpnet, bot_A_mpnet,
           "A6 — all-mpnet-base-v2 (combMIMC_lvl1a)", "A6_mpnet")
save_pairs(top_A_mpnet, bot_A_mpnet, "A6_mpnet")
```

---

## A7 — paraphrase-mpnet-base-v2

**Data**: `combMIMC_lvl1a.csv`  |  **Preprocessing**: `lvl1a` (light)

Fine-tuned for paraphrase detection — identifying when two sentences express the
same meaning in different words. Closest analogue to the MIMC task objective.

```{python a7-para, echo=FALSE}
print("=" * 65)
print("A7 — paraphrase-mpnet-base-v2  (combMIMC_lvl1a)")
print("=" * 65)

cache = f"{output_dir}/embeddings_A_para.npy"
if os.path.exists(cache):
    emb = np.load(cache); print(f"Loaded from cache: {emb.shape}")
else:
    m = SentenceTransformer("sentence-transformers/paraphrase-mpnet-base-v2")
    m.max_seq_length = 512
    emb = m.encode(df_comblvl1a["textMIMC_lvl1a"].tolist(), batch_size=32,
                   show_progress_bar=True, convert_to_numpy=True,
                   normalize_embeddings=True)
    np.save(cache, emb); print(f"Saved: {cache}")

sm_A_para = cosine_similarity(emb)
pairs_A_para          = build_pairs_df(sm_A_para, df_comblvl1a,
                                        "textMIMC_lvl1a", orig_col_comb)
top_A_para, bot_A_para = top_bottom(pairs_A_para)

print(f"\nTOP {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(top_A_para)
print(f"\nBOTTOM {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(bot_A_para)
plot_pairs(top_A_para, bot_A_para,
           "A7 — paraphrase-mpnet-base-v2 (combMIMC_lvl1a)", "A7_para")
save_pairs(top_A_para, bot_A_para, "A7_para")
```

---

## Group A — Cross-Model Comparison

All Group A models share the same 64-document dataset (or a 64-document dataset
with the same row ordering), so pair indices are consistent. TF-IDF is used as
the reference for the side-by-side heatmap as it is the most interpretable baseline.

```{python groupA-cross, echo=FALSE}
model_results_A = {
    "TF-IDF":       (top_A_tfidf,      bot_A_tfidf),
    "W2V-lvl2a":    (top_A_w2v_lvl2a,  bot_A_w2v_lvl2a),
    "W2V-lvl1a":    (top_A_w2v_lvl1a,  bot_A_w2v_lvl1a),
    "BERT":         (top_A_bert,        bot_A_bert),
    "MiniLM":       (top_A_minilm,      bot_A_minilm),
    "mpnet":        (top_A_mpnet,       bot_A_mpnet),
    "para-mpnet":   (top_A_para,        bot_A_para),
}

# Note: TF-IDF and W2V-lvl2a use df_comblvl2a; W2V-lvl1a and sentence models use df_comblvl1a.
# Both have N=64 with the same clip-context ordering, so the pair index (i,j) refers
# to the same clip-context cell in both dataframes.
# Scores are therefore directly lookup-able across models within Group A.
groupA_sim_lookup = {
    "TF-IDF":       sm_A_tfidf,
    "W2V-lvl2a":    sm_A_w2v_lvl2a,
    "W2V-lvl1a":    sm_A_w2v_lvl1a,
    "BERT":         sm_A_bert,
    "MiniLM":       sm_A_minilm,
    "mpnet":        sm_A_mpnet,
    "para-mpnet":   sm_A_para,
}

all_sims_A = run_cross_model(
    model_results_A, groupA_sim_lookup,
    group_label="A",
    ref_key="TF-IDF",
    dist_title="Group A — Similarity Distributions  (combMIMC, N=64 documents)",
    n_cols=4,
)
```

---

# Group B — Individual MIMC (N = all responses)

---

## B1 — TF-IDF

**Data**: `dataMIMC_preprocessed.csv`  |  **Preprocessing**: `lvl2a` (aggressive)

TF-IDF on individual ~10–50 word texts. Most pairs will share zero or very few
words, producing near-uniform low similarity. This demonstrates empirically why
TF-IDF is unsuitable for individual-level analysis.

```{python b1-tfidf, echo=FALSE}
print("=" * 65)
print("B1 — TF-IDF  (individual, lvl2a)")
print("=" * 65)

vectorizer_b = TfidfVectorizer(min_df=1, max_df=1.0, sublinear_tf=True)
tfidf_mat_b  = vectorizer_b.fit_transform(df_indiv["textMIMC_lvl2a"])
sm_B_tfidf   = cosine_similarity(tfidf_mat_b)

pairs_B_tfidf          = build_pairs_df(sm_B_tfidf, df_indiv,
                                         "textMIMC_lvl2a", orig_col_indiv)
top_B_tfidf, bot_B_tfidf = top_bottom(pairs_B_tfidf)

print(f"\nTOP {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(top_B_tfidf)
print(f"\nBOTTOM {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(bot_B_tfidf)
plot_pairs(top_B_tfidf, bot_B_tfidf, "B1 — TF-IDF (individual, lvl2a)", "B1_tfidf")
save_pairs(top_B_tfidf, bot_B_tfidf, "B1_tfidf")
```

---

## B2 — Word2Vec (`lvl2a`)

**Data**: `dataMIMC_preprocessed.csv`  |  **Preprocessing**: `lvl2a` (aggressive)

Word2Vec on individual texts with aggressive preprocessing. Short texts provide
fewer words to average, making the mean vector less stable and representative.

```{python b2-w2v-lvl2a, echo=FALSE}
print("=" * 65)
print("B2 — Word2Vec lvl2a  (individual)")
print("=" * 65)

emb, nz = embed_w2v(df_indiv["textMIMC_lvl2a"], w2v_model)
if nz: print(f"Warning: {nz} zero vectors.")
sm_B_w2v_lvl2a = cosine_similarity(emb)

pairs_B_w2v_lvl2a          = build_pairs_df(sm_B_w2v_lvl2a, df_indiv,
                                              "textMIMC_lvl2a", orig_col_indiv)
top_B_w2v_lvl2a, bot_B_w2v_lvl2a = top_bottom(pairs_B_w2v_lvl2a)

print(f"\nTOP {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(top_B_w2v_lvl2a)
print(f"\nBOTTOM {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(bot_B_w2v_lvl2a)
plot_pairs(top_B_w2v_lvl2a, bot_B_w2v_lvl2a,
           "B2 — Word2Vec lvl2a (individual)", "B2_w2v_lvl2a")
save_pairs(top_B_w2v_lvl2a, bot_B_w2v_lvl2a, "B2_w2v_lvl2a")
```

---

## B3 — Word2Vec (`lvl1a`)

**Data**: `dataMIMC_preprocessed.csv`  |  **Preprocessing**: `lvl1a` (light)

Word2Vec on light-preprocessed individual texts. Comparing B2 vs B3 shows the
preprocessing effect at the individual level. Comparing B3 vs B4 (BERT) isolates
contextualisation on identical individual-level inputs.

```{python b3-w2v-lvl1a, echo=FALSE}
print("=" * 65)
print("B3 — Word2Vec lvl1a  (individual)")
print("=" * 65)

emb, nz = embed_w2v(df_indiv["textMIMC_lvl1a"], w2v_model)
if nz: print(f"Warning: {nz} zero vectors.")
sm_B_w2v_lvl1a = cosine_similarity(emb)

pairs_B_w2v_lvl1a          = build_pairs_df(sm_B_w2v_lvl1a, df_indiv,
                                              "textMIMC_lvl1a", orig_col_indiv)
top_B_w2v_lvl1a, bot_B_w2v_lvl1a = top_bottom(pairs_B_w2v_lvl1a)

print(f"\nTOP {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(top_B_w2v_lvl1a)
print(f"\nBOTTOM {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(bot_B_w2v_lvl1a)
plot_pairs(top_B_w2v_lvl1a, bot_B_w2v_lvl1a,
           "B3 — Word2Vec lvl1a (individual)", "B3_w2v_lvl1a")
save_pairs(top_B_w2v_lvl1a, bot_B_w2v_lvl1a, "B3_w2v_lvl1a")
```

---

## B4 — BERT

**Data**: `dataMIMC_preprocessed.csv`  |  **Preprocessing**: `lvl1a` (light)

BERT with mean-pooling on individual MIMC descriptions. Same architecture as A4
but on individual texts. Comparing A4 vs B4 shows how BERT behaves across scales;
comparing B4 vs B5 (MiniLM) isolates the effect of similarity fine-tuning.

```{python b4-bert, echo=FALSE}
print("=" * 65)
print("B4 — BERT  (individual)")
print("=" * 65)

cache = f"{output_dir}/embeddings_B_bert.npy"
if os.path.exists(cache):
    emb = np.load(cache); print(f"Loaded from cache: {emb.shape}")
else:
    we = st_models.Transformer("bert-base-uncased", max_seq_length=512)
    pl = st_models.Pooling(we.get_word_embedding_dimension(),
                            pooling_mode_mean_tokens=True)
    m  = SentenceTransformer(modules=[we, pl])
    emb = m.encode(df_indiv["textMIMC_lvl1a"].tolist(), batch_size=32,
                   show_progress_bar=True, convert_to_numpy=True,
                   normalize_embeddings=True)
    np.save(cache, emb); print(f"Saved: {cache}")

sm_B_bert = cosine_similarity(emb)
pairs_B_bert          = build_pairs_df(sm_B_bert, df_indiv,
                                        "textMIMC_lvl1a", orig_col_indiv)
top_B_bert, bot_B_bert = top_bottom(pairs_B_bert)

print(f"\nTOP {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(top_B_bert)
print(f"\nBOTTOM {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(bot_B_bert)
plot_pairs(top_B_bert, bot_B_bert, "B4 — BERT (individual)", "B4_bert")
save_pairs(top_B_bert, bot_B_bert, "B4_bert")
```

---

## B5 — all-MiniLM-L6-v2

**Data**: `dataMIMC_preprocessed.csv`  |  **Preprocessing**: `lvl1a` (light)

Fine-tuned for semantic similarity. Comparing B4 vs B5 on identical inputs
isolates the effect of similarity fine-tuning at the individual level.

```{python b5-minilm, echo=FALSE}
print("=" * 65)
print("B5 — all-MiniLM-L6-v2  (individual)")
print("=" * 65)

cache = f"{output_dir}/embeddings_B_minilm.npy"
if os.path.exists(cache):
    emb = np.load(cache); print(f"Loaded from cache: {emb.shape}")
else:
    m = SentenceTransformer("sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2")
    m.max_seq_length = 128
    emb = m.encode(df_indiv["textMIMC_lvl1a"].tolist(), batch_size=64,
                   show_progress_bar=True, convert_to_numpy=True,
                   normalize_embeddings=True)
    np.save(cache, emb); print(f"Saved: {cache}")

sm_B_minilm = cosine_similarity(emb)
pairs_B_minilm          = build_pairs_df(sm_B_minilm, df_indiv,
                                          "textMIMC_lvl1a", orig_col_indiv)
top_B_minilm, bot_B_minilm = top_bottom(pairs_B_minilm)

print(f"\nTOP {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(top_B_minilm)
print(f"\nBOTTOM {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(bot_B_minilm)
plot_pairs(top_B_minilm, bot_B_minilm,
           "B5 — all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (individual)", "B5_minilm")
save_pairs(top_B_minilm, bot_B_minilm, "B5_minilm")
```

---

## B6 — all-mpnet-base-v2

**Data**: `dataMIMC_preprocessed.csv`  |  **Preprocessing**: `lvl1a` (light)

Quality ceiling for the individual-level sentence-embedding models.

```{python b6-mpnet, echo=FALSE}
print("=" * 65)
print("B6 — all-mpnet-base-v2  (individual)")
print("=" * 65)

cache = f"{output_dir}/embeddings_B_mpnet.npy"
if os.path.exists(cache):
    emb = np.load(cache); print(f"Loaded from cache: {emb.shape}")
else:
    m = SentenceTransformer("sentence-transformers/all-mpnet-base-v2")
    m.max_seq_length = 512
    emb = m.encode(df_indiv["textMIMC_lvl1a"].tolist(), batch_size=32,
                   show_progress_bar=True, convert_to_numpy=True,
                   normalize_embeddings=True)
    np.save(cache, emb); print(f"Saved: {cache}")

sm_B_mpnet = cosine_similarity(emb)
pairs_B_mpnet          = build_pairs_df(sm_B_mpnet, df_indiv,
                                         "textMIMC_lvl1a", orig_col_indiv)
top_B_mpnet, bot_B_mpnet = top_bottom(pairs_B_mpnet)

print(f"\nTOP {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(top_B_mpnet)
print(f"\nBOTTOM {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(bot_B_mpnet)
plot_pairs(top_B_mpnet, bot_B_mpnet,
           "B6 — all-mpnet-base-v2 (individual)", "B6_mpnet")
save_pairs(top_B_mpnet, bot_B_mpnet, "B6_mpnet")
```

---

## B7 — paraphrase-mpnet-base-v2

**Data**: `dataMIMC_preprocessed.csv`  |  **Preprocessing**: `lvl1a` (light)

Fine-tuned for paraphrase detection. At the individual level, the paraphrase
objective is especially relevant — different participants describing the same
experience in their own words is structurally a paraphrase task.

```{python b7-para, echo=FALSE}
print("=" * 65)
print("B7 — paraphrase-mpnet-base-v2  (individual)")
print("=" * 65)

cache = f"{output_dir}/embeddings_B_para.npy"
if os.path.exists(cache):
    emb = np.load(cache); print(f"Loaded from cache: {emb.shape}")
else:
    m = SentenceTransformer("sentence-transformers/paraphrase-mpnet-base-v2")
    m.max_seq_length = 512
    emb = m.encode(df_indiv["textMIMC_lvl1a"].tolist(), batch_size=32,
                   show_progress_bar=True, convert_to_numpy=True,
                   normalize_embeddings=True)
    np.save(cache, emb); print(f"Saved: {cache}")

sm_B_para = cosine_similarity(emb)
pairs_B_para          = build_pairs_df(sm_B_para, df_indiv,
                                        "textMIMC_lvl1a", orig_col_indiv)
top_B_para, bot_B_para = top_bottom(pairs_B_para)

print(f"\nTOP {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(top_B_para)
print(f"\nBOTTOM {TOP_N}:"); print_pairs(bot_B_para)
plot_pairs(top_B_para, bot_B_para,
           "B7 — paraphrase-mpnet-base-v2 (individual)", "B7_para")
save_pairs(top_B_para, bot_B_para, "B7_para")
```

---

## Group B — Cross-Model Comparison

All Group B models share the same individual-level dataset. BERT is used as the
reference model for the heatmap so the fine-tuning effect (B4 vs B5) and the
lexical-vs-semantic contrast (B1/B2 vs B4/B5) are both immediately visible.

```{python groupB-cross, echo=FALSE}
model_results_B = {
    "TF-IDF":       (top_B_tfidf,      bot_B_tfidf),
    "W2V-lvl2a":    (top_B_w2v_lvl2a,  bot_B_w2v_lvl2a),
    "W2V-lvl1a":    (top_B_w2v_lvl1a,  bot_B_w2v_lvl1a),
    "BERT":         (top_B_bert,        bot_B_bert),
    "MiniLM":       (top_B_minilm,      bot_B_minilm),
    "mpnet":        (top_B_mpnet,       bot_B_mpnet),
    "para-mpnet":   (top_B_para,        bot_B_para),
}

groupB_sim_lookup = {
    "TF-IDF":       sm_B_tfidf,
    "W2V-lvl2a":    sm_B_w2v_lvl2a,
    "W2V-lvl1a":    sm_B_w2v_lvl1a,
    "BERT":         sm_B_bert,
    "MiniLM":       sm_B_minilm,
    "mpnet":        sm_B_mpnet,
    "para-mpnet":   sm_B_para,
}

all_sims_B = run_cross_model(
    model_results_B, groupB_sim_lookup,
    group_label="B",
    ref_key="BERT",
    dist_title="Group B — Similarity Distributions  (individual MIMC responses)",
    n_cols=4,
)
```

---

## Summary

```{python summary, echo=FALSE}
print("FULL MODEL COMPARISON SUMMARY  (6 models × 2 data levels)")
print("=" * 80)
print(f"\nGroup A: {len(df_comblvl1a)} combMIMC documents  |  "
      f"Group B: {len(df_indiv)} individual responses  |  "
      f"Top/bottom N: {TOP_N}")

header = f"\n  {'Model':<16} {'Preproc':<8}  {'--- Group A (combMIMC) ---':^28}  {'--- Group B (individual) ---':^28}"
print(header)
print(f"  {'':<16} {'':<8}  {'Mean':>6} {'SD':>6} {'Min':>6} {'Max':>6}  "
      f"{'Mean':>6} {'SD':>6} {'Min':>6} {'Max':>6}")
print("  " + "-" * 82)

rows = [
    ("TF-IDF",     "lvl2a", all_sims_A["TF-IDF"],    all_sims_B["TF-IDF"]),
    ("W2V",        "lvl2a", all_sims_A["W2V-lvl2a"], all_sims_B["W2V-lvl2a"]),
    ("W2V",        "lvl1a", all_sims_A["W2V-lvl1a"], all_sims_B["W2V-lvl1a"]),
    ("BERT",       "lvl1a", all_sims_A["BERT"],       all_sims_B["BERT"]),
    ("MiniLM",     "lvl1a", all_sims_A["MiniLM"],     all_sims_B["MiniLM"]),
    ("mpnet",      "lvl1a", all_sims_A["mpnet"],       all_sims_B["mpnet"]),
    ("para-mpnet", "lvl1a", all_sims_A["para-mpnet"], all_sims_B["para-mpnet"]),
]
for name, prep, sA, sB in rows:
    print(f"  {name:<16} {prep:<8}  "
          f"{sA.mean():>6.3f} {sA.std():>6.3f} {sA.min():>6.3f} {sA.max():>6.3f}  "
          f"{sB.mean():>6.3f} {sB.std():>6.3f} {sB.min():>6.3f} {sB.max():>6.3f}")

print(f"\nOutputs saved to: {output_dir}")
print("  pairs_top/bottom_<ID>.csv — pair lists for each of the 14 model runs")
print("  pairs_<ID>.png            — bar charts")
print("  groupA/B_jaccard_top/bottom.png   — agreement heatmaps")
print("  groupA/B_distributions.png        — score distributions")
print("  groupA/B_cross_model_heatmap.png  — side-by-side score heatmaps")
print("  groupA/B_cross_model_scores.csv   — full comparison tables")
```
